raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.3": 0.0,
    "R1.4": 0.0,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 1.0,
    "R1.7": 0.0,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 0.7,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "The request is very broad. Should I prioritize feedback on (A) the low-level writing and grammar (e.g., sentence structure, punctuation), or (B) the high-level conceptual and logical flow of the argument?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The request for general feedback on this dense text implies a need to clarify its core claims and contributions.",
    "R1.2": "Evaluating the text requires checking the coherence of the argument from problem formulation to the convergence claim.",
    "R1.6": "The artifact's run-on sentences and lack of punctuation make sentence-level polishing a load-bearing part of any useful feedback.",
    "RX.1": "The open-ended request requires me to be explicit about the limits of my analysis based only on the provided text.",
    "RX.2": "I must ensure my feedback helps the user calibrate the strength of their claims, a key part of general scholarly advice.",
    "RX.3": "The broad 'what do you think' prompt requires me to strictly limit my response to feedback on the provided text and not speculate further.",
    "RX.4": "My feedback must not introduce technical inaccuracies or misleading suggestions into this research-oriented text.",
    "RX.5": "All feedback must be grounded directly in the provided artifact, as no external context or sources were given."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: The request is very broad. Should I prioritize feedback on (A) the low-level writing and grammar (e.g., sentence structure, punctuation), or (B) the high-level conceptual and logical flow of the argument?

rationale.R1.1: The request for general feedback on this dense text implies a need to clarify its core claims and contributions.
rationale.R1.2: Evaluating the text requires checking the coherence of the argument from problem formulation to the convergence claim.
rationale.R1.6: The artifact's run-on sentences and lack of punctuation make sentence-level polishing a load-bearing part of any useful feedback.
rationale.RX.1: The open-ended request requires me to be explicit about the limits of my analysis based only on the provided text.
rationale.RX.2: I must ensure my feedback helps the user calibrate the strength of their claims, a key part of general scholarly advice.
rationale.RX.3: The broad 'what do you think' prompt requires me to strictly limit my response to feedback on the provided text and not speculate further.
rationale.RX.4: My feedback must not introduce technical inaccuracies or misleading suggestions into this research-oriented text.
rationale.RX.5: All feedback must be grounded directly in the provided artifact, as no external context or sources were given.
